details.description: Close-up, low-angle view of a white masonry lighthouse at golden hour. The cylindrical tower has smooth rendered blockwork, a metal balcony and lantern room with glazing and railings. The attached base building features decorative crenellated parapets and buttress-like pilasters. Above an arched entry is an incised date reading “1901.” The structure is brightly lit by warm, low sun against a clear blue sky, with a glimpse of ocean horizon at right. The scene suggests the lighthouse precinct/forecourt with the photographer standing very near the entrance facade.

details.description: A white fortress-like building with a cylindrical lighthouse rising above it. The base features castellated, battlement-like elements and arches, including a prominent arched doorway. The lantern room sits atop a round tower, encased in glass with metal framing. The facade is pristine white, bathed in warm golden-hour sunlight, with a clear deep-blue sky behind and a hint of the sea to the right. The overall impression is a classic lighthouse on a headland, likely Cape Byron Lighthouse, Byron Bay, built in 1901.

details.description: A low-angle, close-up photograph of the Cape Byron Lighthouse captured during the golden hour, likely at sunrise or sunset, as evidenced by the warm, yellow illumination on the white masonry against a deep blue, gradient sky. The image features the upper portion of the cylindrical lighthouse tower and its attached base structure. The tower is topped with a lantern room featuring a dark metal gallery railing and glass panes, supported by decorative corbelling. The base building exhibits a distinctive architectural style with a castellated (crenellated) parapet, giving it a fortress-like appearance. A key identifying feature is the date "1901" inscribed in black numerals above an arched alcove on the right side of the frame. To the left, a tall, rectangular window with multiple panes is visible set into the white block wall. The texture of the construction blocks is highlighted by the side lighting.

details.description: A low-angle shot of a white masonry lighthouse under a clear blue sky during the golden hour. The lighthouse features a cylindrical tower topped with a glass-enclosed lantern room and a metal gallery. The base of the structure has a castle-like, crenelated parapet with arched entryways and a prominent inscription of the year '1901'. The building is constructed from white-painted concrete blocks, and the warm sunlight creates strong shadows, highlighting its architectural details.

The cylindrical tower rises from a crenellated base building with arched openings and an inscription reading "1901" above an entry. The lantern room with glazing and a gallery sits atop the tower. The scene is under a clear blue sky with warm sunlight highlighting the lighthouse’s crisp details. This is the Cape Byron Lighthouse at Byron Bay, New South Wales, Australia.

details.description: A white cylindrical lighthouse tower with a domed lantern room sits atop a fortress-like white structure with crenellated parapets. The building and tower are bathed in warm, golden light from the low sun, set against a clear deep blue sky. The lantern gallery encircles the top of the tower with glass panes and a railing, while the base features arched openings and masonry details. The scene is consistent with Cape Byron Lighthouse in Byron Bay, New South Wales, Australia, a coastal landmark built in 1901.

details.description: Close, low-angle view of a white masonry lighthouse and attached castellated entry block, lit by warm golden-hour sunlight under a clear blue sky. The cylindrical tower is banded like ashlar, with a glazed lantern and a metal gallery featuring a Greek-key frieze. The adjoining building has crenellations and an arched doorway with the date “1901” inscribed above. A coastal railing and a sliver of ocean are visible at the far right.

details.description: A white cylindrical lighthouse rises from a fortress-like base with crenellated battlements. The lantern room at the top is circular with glass panes and a metal railing around a small gallery, and a dark band partitions the tower near the lantern. The facade features block-like stonework and a prominent arched entrance on the right, above which the year 1901 is engraved. Warm golden hour light from the low sun casts long shadows on the masonry, with a clear deep blue sky and a hint of distant sea on the horizon.

details.description: A low-angle, close-up photograph of the upper section and base of a white lighthouse, bathed in warm, golden-hour sunlight against a deep blue, cloudless sky. The structure features a cylindrical tower topped with a glass-paned lantern room and a dark metal balcony railing supported by decorative corbels. The base of the lighthouse exhibits a fortress-like architectural style with crenellated parapets (battlements) along the roofline. To the right, a projecting entrance annex features an arched doorway with the year "1901" inscribed clearly in black numerals above the arch. On the left, a rectangular window with horizontal panes is set into the white masonry wall. A glimpse of the dark blue ocean horizon is visible in the far lower right background.

details.description: A low-angle, close-up shot of the Cape Byron Lighthouse during the golden hour. The structure is built from white-painted concrete blocks and features a distinctive castle-like base with crenelated parapets. The year '1901' is inscribed in black above a recessed arched entrance. A cylindrical tower rises from this base, leading to a lantern room with a white dome and a dark metal gallery. The lighting is warm and directional, casting soft shadows and highlighting the texture of the masonry against a clear, deep blue sky.

details.description: A white coastal architectural structure featuring a tall cylindrical lighthouse with a glass-paneled lantern room at the top, encircled by a dark brown band and a metal railing. The tower rises from a fortress-like white building with crenellated parapets and arched entryways. A small window is visible on the tower shaft, and the facade bears the year 1901. The scene is bathed in warm, low-angle light, casting long shadows and giving the walls a golden hue, with a deep blue sky and a hint of the sea on the horizon to the right.
